PCS-002.brief:
# Combined Travel + Cargo Action Plan — GySgt BRENNAN

## BLUF
TRAVELOG recommends drive escort as the single integrated PCS mode. Reason: you must personally escort an M1078 LMTV transfer, and HHG at ~12,000 lbs is best moved separately under government-arranged surface shipment. This aligns traveler movement, cargo accountability, and mission timing for a D+30 report date. Use POV/official routing only if specifically authorized in orders; otherwise execute the LMTV movement under unit-directed dispatch/TMR procedures and HHG under PPSO shipment. Travel and reimbursement must follow JTR Ch. 2 and Ch. 3; voucher settlement follows DoDFMR Vol. 9; cargo movement documentation should comply with DTR 4500.9-R.

## Recommended Mode
Drive escort.

Package:
- Escort and deliver the M1078 LMTV from Camp Lejeune to Camp Pendleton under unit movement instructions.
- Ship ~12,000 lbs HHG via government-arranged surface shipment through the local PPSO/DMO.
- Execute PCS en route travel per orders, preserving fuel, lodging, and authorized stop receipts for voucher settlement.
- Coordinate required dispatch, licensing, hazmat/safety checks, and delivery appointment before departure.

Why this mode:
- It is the only option that directly satisfies the escorted tactical vehicle requirement.
- It avoids costly/less practical air movement.
- It preserves HHG movement efficiency while keeping the vehicle under positive control.

## Mode Comparison
- Fly+ship: Fast for member, but does not satisfy escorted LMTV movement.
- Drive+ship: Good for HHG plus POV travel, but still leaves the escorted LMTV requirement unresolved.
- Drive escort: Best fit; integrates PCS travel with required vehicle transfer and standard HHG shipment.
- Fly+air-freight: Highest cost, least practical for 12,000 lbs HHG and tactical vehicle mission.

## DTS Voucher Pre-Fill
- Traveler: GySgt BRENNAN
- PCS Route: MCB Camp Lejeune, NC to MCB Camp Pendleton, CA
- Travel mode: Drive escort per orders
- Depart: D+30 minus authorized travel days
- Reimbursables to track: lodging, authorized fuel/tolls/parking, ferry if applicable, and other order-authorized expenses
- Attachments: PCS orders/amendments, receipts, dispatch/mission documentation as needed
- Settlement authority: JTR Ch. 2/3 and DoDFMR Vol. 9

## TMR Pre-Fill
- Requesting activity: Losing command/transport section
- Cargo/item: 1 x M1078 LMTV transfer
- Origin: MCB Camp Lejeune, NC
- Destination: MCB Camp Pendleton, CA
- Required delivery: NLT report date support window
- Escort: GySgt BRENNAN
- Special instructions: dispatch packet, PMCS complete, licensing verified, fuel/safety equipment on hand, delivery POC confirmed
- Governing reference: DTR 4500.9-R

## Validation
- Confirm orders explicitly authorize escorted vehicle movement and travel days.
- Confirm HHG entitlement/weight allowance supports ~12,000 lbs.
- Validate PPSO pickup window against D+30.
- Validate LMTV roadworthiness, insurance/liability guidance, and command routing.
- Ensure all receipts and mission documents are retained for voucher audit.

PCS-006.brief:
# Combined Travel + Cargo Action Plan — Maj FOSTER

## BLUF
TRAVELOG recommends drive escort as the single integrated PCS mode for this move from MCAS Iwakuni to MCB Camp Butler / Okinawa on D+28. This best aligns with the requirement to escort one government motor-pool item (HMMWV transfer) while separately moving approximately 9,500 lbs of HHG through normal HHG channels. The package supports mission control, custody continuity, and straightforward reimbursement documentation under JTR Ch. 2 and Ch. 3, with voucher settlement under DoDFMR Vol. 9 and cargo movement controls under DTR 4500.9-R.

## Recommended Mode
Drive escort.

Why:
- You have a specific escorted vehicle requirement; this is the decisive factor.
- Escort movement preserves accountability for the HMMWV during inter-island transfer.
- HHG at 9,500 lbs is suitable for standard surface/household goods shipment and does not justify premium air freight.
- This mode minimizes split-custody risk versus fly+ship and avoids unnecessary premium cost versus fly+air-freight.

Execution concept:
- Traveler escorts HMMWV from origin staging to onward sea/authorized surface movement routing into Okinawa.
- HHG ships separately through the PPSO/JPPSO as standard PCS HHG.
- Use official routing, port call, and installation transportation office instructions for the escorted vehicle leg.

## Mode Comparison
- Fly+ship: Good for personal convenience, but weak for required HMMWV escort/control.
- Drive+ship: Better cargo continuity than fly, but still separates member from escorted item at key points.
- Drive escort: Best match for mission need; strongest accountability and operational control.
- Fly+air-freight: Fastest, but least cost-effective and generally unjustified for 9,500 lbs HHG absent urgent mission necessity.

## DTS Voucher Pre-Fill
- Traveler: Maj FOSTER
- PCS Type: OCONUS to OCONUS PCS
- Origin: MCAS Iwakuni, Japan
- Destination: MCB Camp Butler, Okinawa
- Effective travel date: D+28
- Recommended travel mode: Government-directed drive escort
- Reimbursable categories: authorized PCS travel, lodging/per diem as directed, ferry/port/terminal costs, authorized POV/vehicle-related transit costs if ordered, excess baggage only if separately authorized
- Settlement authority: JTR Ch. 2, Ch. 3; DoDFMR Vol. 9
- Required attachments: PCS orders/amendments, receipts, port/ferry documentation, lodging receipts, transportation office endorsements

## TMR Pre-Fill
- Request type: PCS cargo + escorted tactical vehicle movement
- Shipper/Unit: MCAS Iwakuni-originating command
- Consignee: MCB Camp Butler / Okinawa receiving command
- HHG: 9,500 lbs estimated
- Special cargo: 1 x HMMWV, escorted transfer
- Required handling: controlled handoff, port scheduling, escort coordination, receiving inspection
- Mode requested: surface/sea movement with member escort for HMMWV; standard HHG shipment for household goods
- Governing reference: DTR 4500.9-R

## Validation
- Confirm orders explicitly authorize escorted HMMWV movement.
- Verify HHG allowance for grade and dependent status with PPSO under JTR Ch. 5 if needed.
- Confirm no hazardous-material, weapon, or communications-security restrictions apply to the HMMWV shipment.
- Validate port call, staging dates, and Okinawa receiving window before ticketing or dispatch.
- Ensure voucher and cargo documents use identical dates, routing, and shipment identifiers.

PCS-016.brief:
# Combined Travel + Cargo Action Plan — Maj PARK

## BLUF
TRAVELOG recommends fly+ship for this PCS from MCB Camp Butler, Okinawa to MCB Camp Pendleton on D+75. This best balances mission practicality, cost, and compliance for an overseas-to-CONUS move with 10,500 lbs of HHG and one escorted M1083 MTV transfer. Member travel should be arranged by commercial air under PCS orders, while HHG moves by surface shipment through the personal property office, and the MTV moves under separate government-arranged cargo/line-haul coordination. This aligns with JTR Ch. 2 and Ch. 5 travel authorization principles, DoDFMR Vol. 9 reimbursement rules, and DTR 4500.9-R cargo movement controls.

## Recommended Mode
Recommended mode: fly+ship.

Why:
- Okinawa to Camp Pendleton is not a practical POV/drive route.
- Air travel is the standard, most supportable PCS passenger mode for OCONUS-to-CONUS transfer (JTR Ch. 2).
- HHG at 10,500 lbs is suitable for normal government-arranged surface shipment.
- The M1083 MTV should not drive cross-ocean; it requires separate cargo booking, inspection, and movement documentation under DTR 4500.9-R.
- Escort requirement can be supported as official duty if directed in orders; otherwise, escort travel must be specifically justified and authorized.

## Mode Comparison
- fly+ship: Best overall. Lowest administrative friction, PCS-standard passenger movement, compliant cargo handling for HHG and MTV.
- drive+ship: Not feasible for this OCONUS lane; no continuous drivable route.
- drive escort: Not recommended. The MTV cannot be self-driven from Okinawa to California; ocean movement is still required.
- fly+air-freight: Fastest, but excessive cost for 10,500 lbs HHG and generally unjustified absent urgent mission need (JTR cost-effectiveness; DoDFMR Vol. 9).

## DTS Voucher Pre-Fill
- Traveler: Maj PARK
- Trip type: PCS
- From: MCB Camp Butler / Okinawa
- To: MCB Camp Pendleton, CA
- Depart: D+75
- Mode: Commercial air
- Expenses to expect: airfare/TMC ticket, authorized lodging if required, per diem for authorized travel days, baggage if mission-required, taxi/TMC-approved ground transport
- Accounting note: reimburse only authorized PCS travel expenses per DoDFMR Vol. 9 and JTR Ch. 2/3
- Orders note: include statement if MTV escort is directed official duty

## TMR Pre-Fill
- Request type: PCS cargo support
- Origin: MCB Camp Butler / Okinawa
- Destination: MCB Camp Pendleton, CA
- HHG: 10,500 lbs estimated
- Special cargo: 1 x M1083 MTV transfer
- Required actions: vehicle inspection, dimensions/weight verification, hazardous/material certification as applicable, port booking, line-haul coordination, receiving installation coordination
- Escort: Maj PARK only if specifically authorized on orders
- Governing reference: DTR 4500.9-R

## Validation
- Confirm HHG weight is within Maj authorized allowance under JTR.
- Confirm whether unaccompanied baggage is needed in addition to HHG.
- Confirm MTV serial/TCN, dimensions, cube, and readiness status.
- Confirm escort is mandatory and written into orders.
- Confirm required pickup dates support D+75 arrival window.
- Confirm DTS and cargo documents use identical origin/destination and order numbers.
